DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT FCA US, LLC, PUTNAM CHRYSLER JEEP DODGE'S MOTION TO COMPEL VEHICLE INSPECTION AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS [CCP 2031.310, 2023.010].BEFORE THE COURT ARE DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO COMPEL A VEHICLE INSPECTION OF THE 2014 JEEP PATRIOT WHICH IS THE SUBJECT OF THIS ACTION AND FOR $5262.50 IN SANCTIONS AND PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER SEEKING TO PRECLUDE DEFENDANTS FROM CONDUCTING A "SECOND" VEHICLE INSPECTION OF THE JEEP PATRIOT. BOTH MOTIONS ARISE FROM AN EFFORT BY DEFENDANTS TO INSPECT THE VEHICLE ON APRIL 27, 2018. THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION TO COMPEL AND ORDERS THE PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER TO SELECT A DATE FOR AN INSPECTION TO OCCUR WITHIN THE NEXT 20 DAYS AT A PLACE OF DEFENDANTS' SELECTION. DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED FOR FAILURE TO INCLUDE A "DECLARATION SETTING FORTH FACTS SUPPORTING THE AMOUNT OF ANY MONETARY SANCTION SOUGHT." CCP SECTION 2023.040. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL MS. NASSIRIAN TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 DIRECTLY BY EMAIL TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT SKMEDIATE@GMAIL.COM. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Vehicle Inspection And Request For Monetary Sanctions [Ccp 2031.310, 2023.010]
Off Calendar May-31-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Gabriella Pedone, Esq. 310-929-4941 of Strategic Legal Practices for Plaintiff Gianna Galleta through Court Call.
Nejla Nassirian, Esq. 916-780-4050 of Universal & Shannon LLP for Defendant FCA US, LLC, PUTNAM CHRYSLER JEEP DODGE.
Court reporter: David Disbrow, CSR #7768, 415-298-1538, Reported.

Both sides stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT FCA US, LLC, PUTNAM CHRYSLER JEEP DODGE's Motion To Compel Vehicle Inspection And Request For Monetary Sanctions [Ccp 2031.310, 2023.010].

Before the Court are Defendants' Motion to Compel A Vehicle Inspection of the 2014 Jeep Patriot which is the subject of this action and for $5262.50 in sanctions and Plaintiff's motion for a protective order seeking to preclude Defendants from conducting a "second" vehicle inspection of the Jeep Patriot.

Both motions arise from an effort by Defendants to inspect the vehicle on April 27, 2018.
The Court GRANTS the motion to compel and orders the parties to meet and confer to select a date for an inspection to occur within the next 20 days at a place of Defendants' selection. The Court further orders that the protocol laid out in paragraphs 2 and 4-12 of the Court's order in Moshari v. Mercedes Benz (Nassirian Dec., Exh. Q) will govern the conduct of the inspection. Any data acquired from the inspection shall be available for production pursuant to the provisions of CCP section 2034.210 et seq. or upon the deposition of Defendants' expert. Defendants' motion for sanctions is DENIED for failure to include a "declaration setting forth facts supporting the amount of any monetary sanction sought." CCP section 2023.040.

Defendant's Counsel Ms. Nassirian to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 directly by email to the Judge Pro Tem at skmediate@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Sanford Kingsley, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: David Disbrow, CSR #7768, 415-298-1538, Reported. =(302/JPT)
